script: A wall-mounted television is protected by transparent pre-taped masking film whose integrated yellow tape is adhered to the wall above the television.

scenes.keyframe_prompt: Photorealistic vertical commercial keyframe showing the completed protective state of one integrated pre-taped masking-film product. A modern black 55-inch flat-screen television is wall-mounted in a partially renovated living room. One straight horizontal strip of yellow painter's masking tape is firmly adhered directly onto the flat painted wall, approximately 10 centimeters above the television's top edge. A clearly visible strip of bare wall separates the yellow tape from the black television bezel, proving that the tape is on the wall and not on the television. Thin transparent clear PE film is permanently bonded along the lower edge of that yellow tape and descends continuously from the wall, bridges the short wall gap, then drapes over and completely covers the front and sides of the television. The television silhouette remains visible through the transparent film. The film hangs naturally under gravity with fine realistic wrinkles and slightly loose lower corners. Exactly one yellow tape strip, on the wall above the television; no yellow tape touches the television; no tape on side or bottom edges. No people, no hands, no roll, no installation action. Eye-level medium shot, natural daylight, neutral colors, realistic plastic material, clean advertising photography, no text, no logo, no watermark.
scenes.video_prompt: Keep the exact completed wall-anchored protective installation unchanged. The single horizontal yellow masking-tape strip remains visibly adhered to the painted wall about 10 centimeters above the television, with a clear bare-wall gap between tape and black television bezel. The transparent PE film remains permanently connected to the tape's lower edge, descends from the wall, crosses the bare-wall gap, and covers the television continuously. The tape never moves onto or touches the television. No extra tape appears on either side or bottom. The television, wall, tape, and product remain stationary. Only a very slow subtle camera push-in. Faint indoor air produces minimal natural ripples at the loose lower film corners; all other film stays stable and follows gravity. No people, installation, unrolling, pulling, taping, tearing, removal, transformation, object movement, or product structure change. Photorealistic materials and stable lighting.
scenes.negative_prompt: yellow tape on television bezel, tape touching television, tape attached to television, side tape, bottom tape, vertical yellow tape, multiple tape strips, floating tape, detached tape, separate plastic sheet, person, hands, painter, film roll, installation action, unrolling, pulling, tearing, removing, duplicated film, extra plastic layers, film disappearing, film growing, opaque cloth, television deformation, moving television, melting plastic, strong wind, large film motion, flicker, jump cut, camera orbit, text, logo, watermark
